claim_or_surface: `/benchmarks` metadata says reproducible runs, public seeds, 95% CI.
evidence: `public/benchmarks.html` title and OG title include 95% CI.
code_truth: `src/benchmark.js` reports latency percentiles and accuracy, but no confidence interval fields or CI calculation.
risk: Readers can infer statistical support that the artifact harness does not compute.
recommended_action: Remove 95% CI from artifact benchmark metadata or add CI fields to `kolm-benchmark-1`.

claim_or_surface: Artifact-local benchmarks and SWE-bench reproducer share the same benchmark umbrella.
evidence: `/benchmarks` covers `.kolm` fixtures; `/articles/how-we-benchmark` covers SWE-bench Lite with Opus; CLI exposes both under `kolm bench`.
code_truth: The two paths use different harnesses, data, models, costs, and proof standards.
risk: Claims can be transferred from the stronger story to the weaker story.
recommended_action: Split evidence pages and schemas for artifact benchmarks vs SWE-bench reproducer.

claim_or_surface: Local build can reproduce the headline SWE-bench lift.
evidence: `bench/README.md` documents a local Docker build path and headline +10.67pp.
code_truth: `bench/run.py` says the full implementation lives externally and `evaluate_patch` returns `False` for any run.
risk: A public local build can spend API budget and still never reproduce the claimed lift.
recommended_action: Complete local evaluator wiring or label the bundled reproducer as a report-shape sketch only.

claim_or_surface: The CLI runs a pinned Docker image for the published numbers.
evidence: `cli/kolm.js` uses image `kolmogorov/swebench-reproducer:1.0.0` and reports methodology URLs.
code_truth: The repo cannot prove the registry image exists or matches the checked-in sketch without pulling external Docker content.
risk: Operators have to trust an unpublished or mutable external image for the headline.
recommended_action: Publish image digest, provenance, and a local source tree that produces identical reports.

claim_or_surface: SWE-bench methodology uses a single named statistical method.
evidence: `public/articles/how-we-benchmark.html` says Wilson CI and exact McNemar; `bench/README.md` says bootstrap CI and Wilcoxon signed-rank.
code_truth: `bench/run.py` implements bootstrap CI and no p-value calculation in the checked-in sketch.
risk: Statistical language is inconsistent enough to fail technical review.
recommended_action: Choose the paired-test method, implement it in the reproducer, and generate the article numbers from its report.

claim_or_surface: K-score is a 0..1 gate everywhere.
evidence: `public/k-score.html` and `src/artifact.js` describe a 0..1 weighted K-score with gate 0.85.
code_truth: Checked-in fixtures and `sample-bench.json` carry hundreds-scale composites such as 424.57.
risk: CI gates and public tables can compare incompatible K-score generations.
recommended_action: Version K-score schemas and rebuild fixtures or label legacy fixture scores explicitly.

claim_or_surface: Benchmark reports recompute the K-score from observed evals and latency.
evidence: `/benchmarks` presents the harness as rerunning evals and reporting K-score.
code_truth: `src/benchmark.js` reads `bundle.manifest.k_score?.composite`; it does not recompute K-score from the benchmark result.
risk: A stale or legacy manifest score can be reported even when observed benchmark axes differ.
recommended_action: Add recomputed K-score fields or rename manifest score vs observed score.

claim_or_surface: Every `.kolm` carries its own evals.
evidence: `public/benchmarks.html` says every `.kolm` carries evals used to gate it.
code_truth: `src/spec-compile.js` accepts specs without `evals` and creates `{n:0,cases:[]}`.
risk: No-eval signed artifacts are possible through the documented local compile path.
recommended_action: Require non-empty evals for compile or scope the claim to curated fixtures.

claim_or_surface: No eval, no ship.
evidence: `public/k-score.html` uses no-eval/no-ship wording.
code_truth: `compile --spec` defaults `training_stats` to pass_rate_positive 1.0 and can package zero eval cases.
risk: The easiest local authoring path can create an artifact without actual eval evidence.
recommended_action: Add spec validation requiring eval cases and executed pass results before signing.

claim_or_surface: Bad artifacts are never written to disk.
evidence: `public/benchmarks.html` says gate failures produce nothing.
code_truth: `src/artifact.js` computes `k_score.ships` but `buildAndZip` always writes the final zip after computing it.
risk: Builder-level guarantees do not match product copy.
recommended_action: Enforce the gate in `buildAndZip` or move the claim to the synthesis path only.

claim_or_surface: Cloud compile always embeds positive eval cases.
evidence: `src/compile.js` comments say no-eval/no-compile gate.
code_truth: When request examples are empty, synthesis uses a fallback positive but artifact `evals_obj` has `n:0` and `cases:[]`.
risk: Cloud-generated artifacts can complete with no embedded eval cases when callers provide no examples.
recommended_action: Reject compile requests without examples or embed the fallback case and label it as synthetic.

claim_or_surface: The `/benchmarks` sample command and JSON are consistent.
evidence: The sample command uses `kolm bench support.kolm --runs 10`; the sample report shows `graded:400` for 4 evals.
code_truth: `src/benchmark.js` calculates graded as cases times runs, so 4 evals with 10 runs would grade 40, not 400.
risk: Developers can misread the report contract and write wrong docs/tests.
recommended_action: Regenerate the sample block directly from the command shown.

claim_or_surface: Reference latency values have one canonical source.
evidence: Public table, `docs/benchmark-results-v0.1.0.md`, and `test/fixtures/sample-bench.json` publish different sample latencies.
code_truth: Latency varies by hardware, but the docs do not identify a single canonical machine-readable source.
risk: Benchmark comparisons become ambiguous and hard to diff.
recommended_action: Store one report per fixture and generate tables from those reports.

claim_or_surface: All four fixture table rows have checked-in machine-readable reports.
evidence: `/benchmarks` lists four 50-run fixture rows.
code_truth: Only `test/fixtures/sample-bench.json` was found as a report artifact.
risk: The table cannot be independently regenerated from checked-in data for all rows.
recommended_action: Add `*-bench.json` reports for redactor, extractor, and classifier.

claim_or_surface: Tests verify the published 50-run and 100-run reference numbers.
evidence: `tests/artifact-end-to-end.test.js` benchmarks fixtures with `runs:2`; `tests/server.test.js` uses `runs:2` and CLI `runs:1`.
code_truth: Tests cover report shape and pass/egress/signature, not the public table values.
risk: Public benchmark drift can ship while tests stay green.
recommended_action: Add snapshot tests generated from canonical fixture reports.

claim_or_surface: Benchmark CLI smoke is continuously run.
evidence: `scripts/smoke-bench-cli.mjs` is a useful three-command smoke.
code_truth: No workflow reference to that script was found in the checked workflows search.
risk: A broken public benchmark CLI can miss CI unless root tests happen to catch it.
recommended_action: Wire the smoke script or equivalent fixture benchmark into CI.

claim_or_surface: Benchmark egress proof is an OS-level network block.
evidence: `/benchmarks` uses strong sovereignty language around egress proof.
code_truth: `src/benchmark.js` patches selected Node APIs in-process during recipe execution.
risk: The proof is useful but narrower than arbitrary-artifact network isolation.
recommended_action: Describe the control as a Node API egress monitor or add OS/container network isolation.

claim_or_surface: Benchmark egress monitor covers all possible side effects.
evidence: The monitor patches `fetch`, `http`, `https`, `net`, `tls`, and `dns`.
code_truth: It does not cover native binaries, subprocess networking, or OS-level channels; recipe VM limits help but the monitor is not the whole sandbox.
risk: Public-untrusted artifacts need stronger execution policy than benchmark patching.
recommended_action: Tie benchmark claims to sandbox trust levels and malicious egress tests.

claim_or_surface: The four checked-in fixtures are real signed artifacts.
evidence: Local smoke run returned expected bytes/eval counts, all evals passed, signatures true, and egress 0 for all four fixtures.
code_truth: This supports artifact fixture credibility for the recipe tier.
risk: This is a strength, but it should be the explicit scope of the benchmark page.
recommended_action: Keep the fixture smoke and make it the canonical minimum claim.

claim_or_surface: The sample fixture is byte-for-byte reproducible across rebuilds.
evidence: `docs/benchmark-results-v0.1.0.md` opens with byte-for-byte reproducibility language.
code_truth: The same doc later says on-disk zip bytes are not byte-stable because zip mtimes, `created_at`, and `receipt_id` use wall-clock/random values.
risk: Internal benchmark docs contradict themselves.
recommended_action: Use stable wording: captured artifact bytes are hash-verifiable; rebuild content is not byte-stable today.

claim_or_surface: The same spec run a year later produces a byte-identical artifact.
evidence: `docs/PRODUCT.md` makes the year-later byte-identical claim.
code_truth: `src/artifact.js` writes `created_at`, `issued_at`, and random `receipt_id`; benchmark docs acknowledge zip bytes are not byte-stable.
risk: A high-visibility product claim is already disproven by code.
recommended_action: Remove byte-identical wording until deterministic zip metadata and deterministic receipt IDs exist.

claim_or_surface: `kolm compile --gate` exists for safety-critical/prototype thresholds.
evidence: `public/k-score.html` documents `kolm compile --gate 0.95` and `--gate 0.70`.
code_truth: `cli/kolm.js` compile path does not parse a `--gate` flag in the inspected code.
risk: Users cannot apply the documented gate override.
recommended_action: Implement `--gate` or remove it from public K-score examples.

claim_or_surface: MCP discovery refuses every low/no-score artifact below project gate.
evidence: `services/mcp/server.js` skips artifacts only when `typeof k === 'number' && k < src.k_min`.
code_truth: Artifacts with missing or non-numeric K-score are not rejected by that condition.
risk: No-score artifacts can still be exposed under a nonzero project gate.
recommended_action: Treat missing or invalid K-score as below gate when `k_min > 0`.

claim_or_surface: Tune promotion evaluates the candidate adapter behavior.
evidence: Tune docs and CLI copy frame tune promotion as K-score gated.
code_truth: `src/tune.js` comments say eval currently delegates to artifact recipes; adapter only matters in a later feature tier.
risk: The gate can look stronger than the current adapter implementation.
recommended_action: Label tune gating as recipe-eval placeholder until adapters participate in execution.

claim_or_surface: The benchmark page makes no frontier-model performance claim.
evidence: `/benchmarks` says no SWE-bench and no frontier model improvement; `/articles/how-we-benchmark` still publishes Opus/SWE-bench lift.
code_truth: The separation exists but is fragile because both are linked through benchmark navigation and CLI naming.
risk: Buyers can still conflate artifact proof with SWE-bench prompt-injection proof.
recommended_action: Add explicit cross-page disclaimers and separate navigation labels.
